known_gaps: The current page begins in October 2023; earlier API events require launch posts.

sources.event_granularity: every dated deprecation heading in scope
known_gaps: Rows bundle multiple model shutdowns under one announcement event.
Living registry may revise replacement recommendations.
Some cards contain many fixes in one release event.
GitHub package/CLI releases not separately atomized when absent from this page.
Pre-launch prototype changes are not represented.
Patch-only details are summarized rather than emitted as separate nodes.
The page has sparse entries after March 2025 because the product surface changed.
The legacy page stops in January 2025; later desktop changes appear in ChatGPT and Codex notes.
No build notes are listed between March 10 and the July 9 deprecation announcement.

sources.event_granularity: selected material admin, Codex, app and lifecycle sections
known_gaps: The living page has many connector/model parity entries that overlap the main ChatGPT release notes.
Not every low-level admin or connector change is atomized in this lane.

sources.event_granularity: selected plan, admin, Apps SDK and availability milestones
known_gaps: Many feature-parity entries duplicate consumer or Enterprise notes and are left to the corresponding lanes.

sources.event_granularity: selected launches needed to fill changelog gaps
known_gaps: This is not a complete extraction of every OpenAI blog post; only platform/client/business/safety launches in scope were included.

sources.event_granularity: major public security products and governance changes
known_gaps: Research-only security posts and incident responses are excluded unless they changed a public product, framework, or lifecycle.

global_gaps: No single official page contains every OpenAI release across products.
Living changelogs may be rewritten, renamed, consolidated or pruned after publication.
The API changelog currently starts in October 2023, so earlier API milestones are reconstructed from official launch posts and the deprecation registry.
Enterprise/Business notes overlap with the consumer ChatGPT notes; this lane records material administrative and lifecycle changes but not every duplicated feature-parity notice.
Some shutdown dates occur after the research cutoff; those nodes record the announcement date and lifecycle state rather than falsely marking the product already retired.
